confirmed.title: publishing-setup.md meta-package member list omits arqel-dev/widgets
confirmed.suggested_fix: Change the list to '... core + auth + fields + form + actions + nav + table + widgets + inertia.' to match packages/arqel/composer.json require keys.
confirmed.detail: Line 36 states `composer require arqel-dev/framework` puxa 'core + auth + fields + form + actions + nav + table + inertia' — eight members. The actual meta-package require block in packages/arqel/composer.json lists arqel-dev/core, auth, fields, form, actions, nav, table AND arqel-dev/widgets (plus inertiajs/inertia-laravel). 'widgets' is a real require of the framework metapackage but is missing from this enumeration. (Note: the sibling installation.md omission was already fixed in a prior round, but this docs/publishing-setup.md instance is a separate, untouched surface.)
confirmed.why_real: Confirmed both sides. docs/publishing-setup.md:36 states `composer require arqel-dev/framework` puxa "core + auth + fields + form + actions + nav + table + inertia" — enumerating 7 Arqel packages plus inertia, omitting widgets. The actual meta-package require block in packages/arqel/composer.json (lines 29-39) lists arqel-dev/core, auth, fields, form, actions, nav, table, AND arqel-dev/widgets, plus inertiajs/inertia-laravel. The package's own description (line 4) also names "(core, auth, fields, form, actions, nav, table, widgets)". So widgets is a genuine, current require of the framework metapackage that is missing from this doc's enumeration — an objectively false/incomplete factual claim. In-scope: docs/publishing-setup.md is a real human-maintained doc (not PLANNING, not docs/tickets, not superpowers, not CHANGELOG, not generated). Not in ledger: the ledger contains docs/publishing-setup.md entries at line 27 and line 333, and an installation.md widgets-omission at apps/docs/guide/installation.md:36 — but NOT this docs/publishing-setup.md:36 widgets-omission, which is a separate untouched surface.

confirmed.title: realtime SKILL.md 'três blocos' undercounts config blocks (omits workflow block)
confirmed.suggested_fix: Change 'expõe três blocos' to 'expõe cinco blocos' and append the missing block: '... e `workflow.broadcast_state_transitions` (env `ARQEL_REALTIME_WORKFLOW_BROADCAST`, default `true`, rebroadcast de transições de state quando arqel-dev/workflow está instalado).'
confirmed.detail: Line 23 says config/arqel-realtime.php 'expõe três blocos' (exposes three blocks), then inline enumerates four (connection, channel_prefix, auto_dispatch, presence). The real config file packages/realtime/config/arqel-realtime.php has FIVE top-level keys: connection, channel_prefix, auto_dispatch, presence, AND workflow (workflow.broadcast_state_transitions, env ARQEL_REALTIME_WORKFLOW_BROADCAST). The 'workflow' block is entirely absent from this 'Config publicável' summary, and the 'três' count is wrong even against the four it lists inline.
confirmed.why_real: Confirmed both sides. packages/realtime/SKILL.md:23 states config/arqel-realtime.php "expõe três blocos" (exposes three blocks). The actual config file packages/realtime/config/arqel-realtime.php has FIVE top-level keys: connection, channel_prefix, auto_dispatch, presence, and workflow (workflow.broadcast_state_transitions, env ARQEL_REALTIME_WORKFLOW_BROADCAST). The doc's inline enumeration lists only four and entirely omits the workflow block, so the "Config publicável" summary is both numerically wrong ("três" vs four listed/five real) and materially incomplete. In-scope (package SKILL.md). Ledger entries for this file concern other claims (nonexistent hooks/ConnectionStatusBanner), not this block-count claim, so not a duplicate.

confirmed.title: core SKILL.md cross-refs a non-existent guide apps/docs/guide/telemetry.md
confirmed.suggested_fix: Remove the dangling '`apps/docs/guide/telemetry.md`' reference, or create the telemetry guide. Telemetry config (`telemetry.enabled`, `metrics_endpoint_*`) does live in packages/core/config/arqel.php, so the simplest fix is to drop the doc pointer or point it at the config block / SKILL section instead.
confirmed.detail: Line 476 states: 'Integrações com Prometheus/Grafana/Datadog/Sentry documentadas em `apps/docs/guide/telemetry.md`.' That file does not exist. `ls apps/docs/guide/` shows no telemetry.md (and `find apps/docs -iname '*telemetry*' -o -iname '*observ*' -o -iname '*metric*'` returns nothing). The guide dir has a11y.md, actions.md, agents.md, auth.md, authentication.md, contributing.md, fields.md, getting-started.md, installation.md, mcp-server.md, panels.md, realtime-collab.md, release-checklist.md, resources.md, tables-forms.md, theming.md, tutorial-first-crud.md, what-is-arqel.md — no telemetry guide anywhere in the corpus. This is the only doc reference to telemetry.md (the only other repo hit is inside packages/marketplace/vendor/, out of scope).
confirmed.why_real: Verified both sides. packages/core/SKILL.md:476 states: 'Integrações com Prometheus/Grafana/Datadog/Sentry documentadas em `apps/docs/guide/telemetry.md`.' This presents the file as existing documentation (a broken cross-reference, not a forward-looking/PLANNING statement). On disk, `ls apps/docs/guide/` lists 18 .md files plus a migration/ dir but NO telemetry.md, and `find apps/docs -iname '*telemetry*'` returns nothing. The doc is in-scope (a package SKILL.md, not PLANNING/docs-tickets/CHANGELOG/generated), the claim is objectively checkable, and it genuinely contradicts current disk state. Not present in reports/docs-fixed-ledger.json.

confirmed.title: cli SKILL.md references apps/docs/laravel-cloud/README.md but the index file is index.md
confirmed.detail: Line 126 lists the Laravel Cloud case-study docs as: '`README.md` (índice), `deploy-guide.md` ..., `auto-scaling.md` ..., `cost-estimation.md` ..., `comparison-other-hosts.md`'. The directory apps/docs/laravel-cloud/ actually contains index.md (not README.md), deploy-guide.md, auto-scaling.md, cost-estimation.md, comparison-other-hosts.md. `ls apps/docs/laravel-cloud/README.md` => no such file. The VitePress config (apps/docs/.vitepress/config.ts) correctly links the overview as `/laravel-cloud/` (i.e. index.md), confirming index.md is the real index file; only this SKILL line names it README.md.
confirmed.why_real: Confirmed both sides. packages/cli/SKILL.md:126 lists the Laravel Cloud case-study docs as "`README.md` (índice), `deploy-guide.md` ..., `auto-scaling.md` ..., `cost-estimation.md` ..., `comparison-other-hosts.md`". The actual directory apps/docs/laravel-cloud/ contains index.md (not README.md), plus deploy-guide.md, auto-scaling.md, cost-estimation.md, comparison-other-hosts.md; `ls .../README.md` returns "No such file or directory". The VitePress config (apps/docs/.vitepress/config.ts:553) links the overview as `/laravel-cloud/`, which resolves to index.md, confirming index.md is the true index. The SKILL line naming it README.md is objectively false. In-scope (packages/cli/SKILL.md, not PLANNING/tickets/CHANGELOG/generated). Not in the ledger: the only cli/SKILL.md ledger entry is line 142 about the artisan command name `arqel:resource:make`, a different claim.

confirmed.title: workflow JS SKILL.md claims package exports 'only <StateTransition>' but WorkflowVisualizer is also exported
confirmed.suggested_fix: Update line 6 to state the package exports both <StateTransition> and <WorkflowVisualizer> (e.g. 'exporta <StateTransition> e <WorkflowVisualizer>'), removing the 'apenas'. Optionally align packages-js/workflow/README.md line 5, which lists only StateTransition and omits WorkflowVisualizer.
confirmed.detail: The Purpose section states: 'Por enquanto exporta apenas <StateTransition>, espelhando o PHP Arqel\Workflow\Fields\StateTransitionField'. This 'apenas' (only) claim is false. packages-js/workflow/src/index.ts exports a second top-level component, WorkflowVisualizer (plus buildMermaidSource, slugifyStateId and several WorkflowVisualizer* types), and the very same SKILL.md documents <WorkflowVisualizer> at line 91+ with usage examples. So the package no longer exports only StateTransition.
confirmed.why_real: Confirmed both sides. packages-js/workflow/SKILL.md lines 5-6 state "Por enquanto exporta apenas `<StateTransition>`" (for now exports ONLY StateTransition). This is objectively false: packages-js/workflow/src/index.ts contains a second export block re-exporting WorkflowVisualizer, buildMermaidSource, slugifyStateId and several WorkflowVisualizer* types from './WorkflowVisualizer.js', alongside the StateTransition exports. The contradiction is self-evident within the same file, which documents <WorkflowVisualizer> with full usage examples at lines 89-139. The doc is in-scope (a package SKILL.md, not PLANNING/tickets/superpowers/CHANGELOG/generated), the claim is checkable, and it genuinely contradicts the code on disk. Not present in reports/docs-fixed-ledger.json (grep for 'apenas', the file path, and the claim returned nothing relevant).

confirmed.title: theme JS SKILL.md shows preventFlashScript() as a PHP/Blade call \Arqel\Theme\preventFlashScript(); no such PHP namespace/function exists
confirmed.suggested_fix: Replace the misleading PHP-FQCN Blade snippet with the real React usage `<script dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{ __html: preventFlashScript() }} />` (the alternate form already shown in src/preventFlash.ts's doc comment), or otherwise make clear preventFlashScript() is JS-side and its returned string must be embedded, not invoked as a PHP function.
confirmed.detail: Under the preventFlashScript(options?) contract, the Blade example is `<script>{!! \Arqel\Theme\preventFlashScript() !!}</script>`. preventFlashScript is a TypeScript export of @arqel-dev/theme (re-exported from @arqel-dev/react/providers, see packages-js/theme/src/index.ts and src/preventFlash.ts) — a JS function returning an IIFE string. There is no `Arqel\Theme` PHP namespace anywhere in packages/ (grep for `namespace Arqel\Theme` returns nothing; there is no packages/theme PHP package). A Blade template cannot call this TS function via a PHP FQCN, so the example is invalid.
confirmed.why_real: Confirmed both sides on disk. SKILL.md:56 documents the preventFlashScript() contract with a Blade example `<script>{!! \Arqel\Theme\preventFlashScript() !!}</script>`, presenting it as a PHP/Blade-callable function via FQCN \Arqel\Theme\. In reality preventFlashScript is a TypeScript export of @arqel-dev/theme: src/index.ts:2 exports it from ./preventFlash, which re-exports from @arqel-dev/react/providers (src/preventFlash.ts:12). It returns an IIFE string for dangerouslySetInnerHTML. grep for `namespace Arqel\Theme` across packages/ returns nothing and there is no packages/theme PHP package (ls confirms absent). A Blade template cannot call this JS function via a nonexistent PHP namespace, so the example is objectively invalid/misleading. In-scope (package SKILL.md, not PLANNING/tickets/CHANGELOG/generated), checkable, and not present in reports/docs-fixed-ledger.json.
